canonical_definition: MUSIC-AVQA is an open-ended VideoQA dataset used to evaluate the framework on multimodal question answering.
papers: CREMA: Generalizable and Efficient Video-Language Reasoning via Multimodal Modular Fusion

relationships.paper: CREMA: Generalizable and Efficient Video-Language Reasoning via Multimodal Modular Fusion
relationships.evidence: "We validate CREMA on various video-language reasoning benchmarks assisted by diverse modalities, including ... Audio-VideoQA (MUSIC-AVQA (Li et al., 2022), VG-GSound (Chen et al., 2020))"

relationships.paper: Sample then Identify: A General Framework for Risk Control and Assessment in Multimodal Large Language Models
relationships.evidence: We consider two multiple-choice VideoQA datasets: Video-MME (VMME) (Fu et al., 2024) and NExT-QA (Xiao et al., 2021)), and two open-ended VideoQA datasets (e.g., MUSIC-AVQA (Li et al., 2022a) and MSVD (Chen & Dolan, 2011))
